verified_title: Comprehensive Identification of Protein Substrates of the Dot/Icm Type IV Transporter of Legionella pneumophila
note: Zhu 2011 BlaM genome-wide Dot/Icm translocation screen; 'Ceg' naming originates here. Guo 2013 (10.1016/j.micinf.2013.11.007) independently confirms Ceg14/Lpg0437 is a Dot/Icm substrate. Locus lpg0437 confirmed.

verified_title: Comprehensive Identification of Protein Substrates of the Dot/Icm Type IV Transporter of Legionella pneumophila
note: Zhu 2011 comprehensive Dot/Icm BlaM translocation screen is the standard primary; could not confirm lpg2422 is among the 275 listed substrates without the supplement. Verify locus against Zhu suppl table before finalizing.

verified_title: Comprehensive Identification of Protein Substrates of the Dot/Icm Type IV Transporter of Legionella pneumophila
note: Zhu 2011 Dot/Icm translocation screen as standard primary; lpg0945 membership in the 275 substrates not confirmed without supplement. Input also flags locus ambiguity (lpg0945=LegL1 vs lpg1290) - resolve locus first.

Current ref resolves to wrong paper (measles). Could not locate via PubMed a primary translocation assay for LpdA/lpg1888 (Schroeder/Viner PLD effector). LpdA is a phospholipase D effector; needs manual lookup of the Schroeder/Viner characterization paper that also shows Dot/Icm delivery.

note: Zhu 2011 Dot/Icm translocation screen as standard primary; lpg2391 membership in the 275 substrates not confirmed without supplement. 'Mav' naming = de Felipe 2005/2008. Verify locus and consider SdbC synonymy.

verified_title: A new method to determine in vivo interactomes reveals binding of the Legionella pneumophila effector PieE to multiple rab GTPases
note: Mousnier/Schroeder 2014 mBio characterizes PieE (lpg1924) as a 69-kDa Dot/Icm T4SS effector and studies it during infection (BirA in-vivo interactome); states Dot/Icm translocation but is not itself a de-novo reporter translocation assay. Protein/locus match confirmed. Stronger primary than Patel 2024 catalog.

note: Zhu 2011 Dot/Icm translocation screen as standard primary; lpg1959 membership in the 275 substrates not confirmed without supplement. Verify locus against Zhu suppl table.

verified_title: Comprehensive Identification of Protein Substrates of the Dot/Icm Type IV Transporter of Legionella pneumophila
note: Zhu 2011 Dot/Icm translocation screen as standard primary; lpg2580 membership in the 275 substrates not confirmed without supplement. 'Sid' naming originates from Luo & Isberg 2004 (PNAS) which may be a better SidL-specific primary if it lists SidL - verify.

verified_title: Identification of Coxiella burnetii type IV secretion substrates required for intracellular replication and Coxiella-Containing Vacuole formation
note: Weber 2013 (matches input hint PMID 23813730) is the TEM-1 BlaM Dot/Icm translocation screen; 53 confirmed substrates; 'Cir' naming (incl CirD/CBU0937) originates here. Could not byte-confirm CBU0937 in the 53-substrate list without supplement but naming and input hint align. Old ref (Fu 2022) was about CirB - correctly flagged wrong.

No VirB-translocation assay for TcpB/Btp1 (BMEI1674) found in PubMed. Salcedo 2008 (10.1371/journal.ppat.0040021) shows Btp1 TIR/DC-maturation function but not T4SS translocation. Marchesini 2011 Brucella VirB substrate screen did NOT recover TcpB as T4SS-dependent. TcpB T4SS-dependence genuinely unestablished; current Felix 2014 ref is TIR structure, not translocation. Manual judgment needed - may be no valid translocation primary.

verified_title: A bipartite signal mediates the transfer of type IV secretion substrates of Bartonella henselae into human cells
note: Schulein 2005 (matches input hint) identifies BepA-G incl BepF as VirB/VirD4-translocated effectors via reporter (Cre/Bla) fusions into human endothelial cells. Direct VirB-dependent translocation demonstrated. BH13430/BepF.

verified_title: A bipartite signal mediates the transfer of type IV secretion substrates of Bartonella henselae into human cells
note: Schulein 2005 identifies BepA-G incl BepG as VirB/VirD4-translocated effectors via reporter-fusion assay. Input flags UniProt label 'BepF protein' on BH13440 but locus matches BepG; Schulein 2005 covers the full BepA-G set so the citation is correct regardless of the Bep name ambiguity.

Same as R179: no VirB-translocation assay for TcpB/Btp1 in B. abortus (BAB1_0279). Marchesini 2011 Brucella VirB screen did not recover TcpB as T4SS-dependent; Salcedo 2008 is functional only. Current Felix 2014 ref is TIR structure. T4SS-dependence unestablished - manual judgment needed.

verified_title: In search of Brucella abortus type IV secretion substrates: screening and identification of four proteins translocated into host cells through VirB system
note: LOCUS-EXACT MATCH: Marchesini 2011 CyaA-reporter screen confirms BAB2_0123 (this row's locus) requires functional VirB T4SS for delivery into host cells. Better than input-hinted de Jong 2008, whose VceC = BAB1_1058 (chr 1), a different locus. NOTE name tangle: 'VceC' in de Jong = BAB1_1058; this row's BAB2_0123 is a distinct VirB substrate. de Jong 2008 (10.1111/j.1365-2958.2008.06487.x) is the alternative if the answer-key intends the BAB1_1058 VceC.

verified_title: Mutational analysis of the Bordetella pertussis fim/fha gene cluster: identification of a gene with sequence similarities to haemolysin accessory genes involved in export of FHA
note: Willems 1994 identifies fhaC as essential for FHA (FhaB) export and shows the FhaB N-terminus is required for translocation across the OM (TPS/T5bSS). Founding FhaB/FhaC secretion primary. Replaces the 2012 prodomain-conformation paper. Protein/subtype/genome all match.

verified_title: Structure-function analysis of the self-recognizing Antigen 43 autotransporter protein from Escherichia coli
note: Klemm 2004 structure-function study of Ag43 (flu) autotransporter: passenger (alpha) + transporter (beta) modules, surface display/self-recognition. Solid T5aSS autotransporter secretion primary. Replaces the 2022 BBRC periplasmic-QC paper. (Founding Henderson/Owen Ag43 AT paper also exists if an earlier ref is preferred.)

verified_title: Maturation and secretion of the non-typable Haemophilus influenzae HMW1 adhesin: roles of the N-terminal and C-terminal domains
note: Grass & St Geme 2000: HMW1A-specific maturation and secretion (translocation across OM via HMW1B translocator, TPS/T5bSS TpsA cargo). Directly on HMW1A, not the HMW1B pore. Replaces the JBC 2006 HMW1B paper.

verified_title: Nucleotide sequencing of the Proteus mirabilis calcium-independent hemolysin genes (hpmA and hpmB) reveals sequence similarity with the Serratia marcescens hemolysin genes (shlA and shlB)
note: Uphoff & Welch 1990: HpmB necessary for extracellular secretion and activation of HpmA; confirms leader cleavage of secreted HpmA (TPS/T5bSS). Defines HpmA as a TpsA substrate. Better than the 1997 cross-complementation paper.

verified_title: Gene structure and extracellular secretion of Neisseria gonorrhoeae IgA protease
note: Pohlner 1987 (matches input hint): founding autotransporter paper - defines the IgA1 protease C-terminal 'helper'/beta-barrel pore mediating extracellular secretion (the original T5aSS mechanism). Note: organism here is N. gonorrhoeae MS11, whereas this row is N. meningitidis MC58; IgA1 protease AT mechanism is conserved across Neisseria. Optionally pair with a full-length accession (Q51163 is a 496aa fragment).

verified_title: Type VI Secretion System Transports Zn2+ to Combat Multiple Stresses and Host Immunity
note: Wang 2015 demonstrates T6SS-4 (Y. pseudotuberculosis) translocates the Zn-binding effector YezP = YPK_3549 (functions extracellularly; confirmed by follow-up 10.1128/aem.00240-23). LOCUS MISMATCH: this row is YPK_3548, one locus off from the demonstrated substrate YPK_3549. Best available T6SS-4 secretion primary but does not directly show YPK_3548 export - verify whether answer-key locus should be YPK_3549. Replaces deleted UniProt A0A0H3B6D1.
